sync_type: Unidirectional or bidirectional (via middleware or native Zapier/Make)
popularity: medium-high — Salesforce for enterprise sales, ActiveCampaign for SMB email automation. Common in organizations that outgrew ActiveCampaign's CRM.

authentication_requirements.salesforce_side: OAuth 2.0 JWT Bearer. Integration user with API Enabled + Read on Contact, Lead, Account.
authentication_requirements.activecampaign_side: API Token (Api-Key header). Admin-level permissions for contact, tag, list, and automation management.

common_use_cases: Salesforce Leads → ActiveCampaign contacts with tags for automation
Deal stage 'Closed Won' → ActiveCampaign list add + automation exit
Salesforce campaign membership → ActiveCampaign automation trigger
ActiveCampaign email engagement data (opens, clicks) → Salesforce as custom activities
Lead scoring sync: ActiveCampaign score → Salesforce numeric field for reporting

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: ActiveCampaign POST /contactTags requires {'contactTag':{'contact':'1','tag':'2'}} wrapper. Flat payload returns 400.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Wrap tag payload in 'contactTag' object. Always test with single contact before bulk operations.

edge_cases_and_errors.error_code: Salesforce daily API limit exhausted by AC sync
edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Bidirectional sync with ActiveCampaign consumes Salesforce API calls. Combined with other integrations, daily allocation exhausted by mid-day.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use Salesforce Bulk API 2.0 for initial sync. Schedule incremental syncs in off-peak hours. Set field-level sync filters to reduce call volume.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Salesforce picklist values mapped to ActiveCampaign dropdown fields don't match. Salesforce boolean not handled by ActiveCampaign checkbox.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Map picklist options explicitly. Use ActiveCampaign 'text' field type as fallback for unknown field types.

edge_cases_and_errors.error_code: Deleted Salesforce records not removed from AC
edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Contact or Lead deleted in Salesforce is not automatically removed from ActiveCampaign. Stale contacts accumulate.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use Salesforce Recycle Bin monitoring or webhook on delete operations. POST to ActiveCampaign /contacts/{id}/delete on delete detection.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Every contact update from Salesforce re-triggers ActiveCampaign automation if 'contact updated' trigger is active. Users receive duplicate emails.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Use ActiveCampaign 'contact added to list' trigger instead of 'contact updated'. Or add a 'skip automation' tag that automations check for.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: Salesforce stores phone as (555) 123-4567. ActiveCampaign normalizes to +15551234567. Re-sync flips between formats.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Choose one format as canonical. Configure ActiveCampaign phone field as text to prevent auto-formatting. Or accept AC's normalization.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: ActiveCampaign uses 'list' as the primary container; Salesforce uses 'campaign.' A contact added to Salesforce campaign should go to AC list, but mapping isn't 1:1.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Map Salesforce Campaign Member records to AC list add via middleware. One Salesforce campaign = one AC list name. Use AC tags for sub-segmentation.

edge_cases_and_errors.cause: ActiveCampaign email open → webhook updates Salesforce contact → Salesforce syncs back to AC → AC records another activity → infinite loop.
edge_cases_and_errors.solution: Track 'source' field on records. AC-created activities get source='ActiveCampaign'. Exclude source=ActiveCampaign records from AC→SF sync.

community_solutions.problem: ActiveCampaign API pagination limits — 20 per page default
community_solutions.solution: Set ?limit=100 on AC list endpoints. For full sync, iterate with ?offset parameter.
Use separate API keys/connections for sandbox and production. AC doesn't differentiate environments.
